title: Developments in Africa and Europe
description: This exercise will focus on the developments in GOV, CDI, and SIO in Africa and Europe, assuming students are familiar with all themes except SIO. It will have 4 types based on the learning objectives below, scaling 3x (total 12).

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**The Swahili architectural features shown in the image above are an effect of which of the following processes from 1200 to 1450 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: An intricately carved wooden door with traditional patterns.
options.caption: Source: Traditional Swahili doorway with Arabic inscriptions, found in Lamu, Kenya. CC BY-SA 2.0

choices.content: the mixing of Bantu-speaking communities with the Islamic religion
the dominance of Islamic caliphates in the region
the diffusion of East Asian culture throughout the Swahili coast
the trading of goods along the Swahili coast with European merchants

assessment_items.question_content:
**States in the Sahel region of West Africa shared several common elements as Islam spread through the region between 900 and 1100 CE. Which of the following illustrates an aspect of the social structure that remained consistent in much of the Sahel region through this time period?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: tracing ancestry through the mother
building temples dedicated to kings
adopting Islamic religious practices
trade of gold across the regional network

assessment_items.question_content:
**The state of Great Zimbabwe dominated a large territory in southern Africa that was rich in minerals and gold by around 1200 CE. Which of the following is the best example of a continued practice that helped rulers maintain regional power?   
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: securing the loyalty of provincial chiefs by loaning cattle and bulls
trading gold with Swahili merchants
gaining legitimacy by adopting Islamic religious practices
maintaining aspects of matrilineal cultural practices

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**The church pictured above best represents which of the following developments in Afro-Eurasia from 1200 to 1450 CE?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: The exterior of a church featuring rock-cut architecture.
options.caption: Source: This is an image of Bete Giyorgis (or the Church of Saint George), which is one of eleven rock-hewn monolithic churches in Lalibela, Ethiopia. These Ethiopian Orthodox churches were commissioned by King Gebre Mesqel Lalibela of the Zagwe dynasty around 1200 CE. CC BY-SA 3.0.

choices.content: religious and political power were often linked
religious leaders paid for the favor of local rulers
religious institutions used government support to spread
religious power overshadowed political power

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows:
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following represents a reasonable inference that a historian would make from the depiction of Mansa Musa above? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A king on a throne is shown holding a gold nugget and wearing a European-style crown.
options.caption: Source: Depiction of Mansa Musa, ruler of the Mali Empire in the 14th century, from a Catalan Atlas of the known world, drawn by Abraham Cresques of Mallorca in 1375 CE. Public domain.

choices.content: His wealth was renowned in Afro-Eurasia.
His empire had conquered parts of Spain by 1350 CE.
His travel writings were read widely by 1400 CE.
His exploitation of the feudal system allowed him to gain wealth.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.
**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Using the image above and your knowledge of world history, which of the following illustrates how the rulers of the Mali Empire gained wealth? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A king on a throne is shown holding a gold nugget and wearing a European-style crown.
options.caption: Source: Depiction of Mansa Musa, ruler of the Mali Empire in the 14th century, from a Catalan Atlas of the known world, drawn by Abraham Cresques of Mallorca in 1375 CE. Public domain.

choices.content: control of trade in the region
conquering West African states
requiring tribute from neighbors
converting to Islam

assessment_items.question_content:
**What development could a historian use to support the claim that Europe was a “world of villages” in 1200 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the manorial system
the feudal system
the shogunate
the encomienda system

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.**

> We, therefore, wishing to put a stop to such iniquitous [sinful] acts, by the counsel of our brothers, of the apostolic authority, have decreed the following: Whatever prelates [high-ranking clergy members], or ecclesiastical [church] persons monastic or secular ... [who] shall pay, or promise, or agree to pay as levies or [land taxes] to laymen ... part of their own and their churches’ revenues or goods ... under the name of an aid, loan, [grant], subsidy or gift ... without the authority of that same chair: likewise emperors, kings or princes, dukes, counts or barons ... who shall impose, exact or receive such payments… shall incur, by the act itself [of taking money from the church], the sentence of excommunication ...

From "Clericis laicos" by Pope Boniface VIII, written in Rome around 1296. It is significant because it made a statement to the monarchs and aristocrats of France and England asserting that they could not take church revenues without the pope’s permission.

**The process described in the above excerpt was most likely an effect of which of the following developments? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the feudal system
the Great Schism
the Medieval Climate Anomaly
the Crusades

assessment_items.question_content:
**Between 1200 and 1450 CE, the feudal system meant that there was no central government in Europe. How did rulers gain and maintain power in this fractured system? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: through the threat of violence
through kinship ties
by requiring tribute
by cooperating with peasants

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the late 1340s, an epidemic known as the Black Death (or bubonic plague) swept across Europe, causing up to half the population to die of the disease. What was the long-term impact of this epidemic in Europe during the 1400s? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: political instability and wars
widespread sickness and disease
the germ theory of disease
exploration for new trade routes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the European and Mediterranean political structure between 1200 and 1450 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: fragmented and determined by a decentralized authority
centralized and built on strong national monarchies
fragmented but secured through confederacies of nations.
centralized but run by local officials in a bureaucracy.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following illustrates how the Black Death shaped social structures in Europe? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: greater social mobility and changes in the feudal system
the development of public health systems and hospitals
increased influence of the Catholic Church
reforms that moved toward equality and representative government
